body:
This is the first part of  #686.

Note that the `ghost_neighboring_elements` may seem a bit odd (it's the local indices which neighbor a ghost element): the rationale here is that it is probably more efficient to use a different iteration pattern. Currently we do
```
for e in elements
  # compute min/max in element e
  for n in neighbors
    # compute min/max in neighbors e
  end
  out_min[e] = min(min_e, minimum(nbrs_min_e))
end
```
which requires multiple passes over all the nodes

Instead, it is probably better to do
```
for e in elements
   # compute extrema in e
   out_min[e] = min(out_min[e], min_e)
   for n in neighbors
         out_min[n] = min(out_min[n], min_e)
   end
end
```

title: define neighbor iterator

body:

body:
Adds a limiter object which stores the necessary buffers. Applying the limiter is now
split across two operations: one to compute the bounds, another to apply the limiter.

Builds on #697.
